title: What problem does Docker solve?
prompt_md:
Your code runs perfectly on your laptop but crashes on a teammate's machine and again on the production server, each time with different library/version errors.

What core problem does Docker solve here?
options: It makes Python code run faster at runtime
It packages the app with its entire environment so it runs the same everywhere — ending 'works on my machine'
It automatically writes your unit tests for you
It encrypts your source code so it can't be copied
explanation_md: Docker bundles your app together with its OS libraries, language runtime, and dependencies into an image. A container started from that image behaves identically on every machine — laptop, CI, and production — which eliminates the 'works on my machine' class of bugs. It isn't about speed, test generation, or encryption.
